description_long: This object is a section of a stone wall constructed from rectangular, rough-hewn blocks with visible mortar lines. The wall segment features a stepped profile, with one end higher than the other, suggesting it is part of a ruined or incomplete barrier. The surface of the stones is uneven and textured, with a dark gray coloration and lighter highlights along the mortar joints, giving it a weathered, aged appearance. The block arrangement is irregular, with some stones protruding slightly, enhancing the rugged look. The structure is solid and heavy, typical of stone masonry used for urban barriers or retaining walls. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements, emphasizing its utilitarian function.
description: A rugged, stepped stone wall segment made of dark gray, rough-hewn blocks with visible mortar lines and a weathered appearance.
description_view_0: A narrow end view showing the thickness of the wall and the stacked stone blocks.
description_view_1: A side view revealing the stepped profile and the irregular arrangement of the stone blocks.
description_view_2: The opposite narrow end view, similar to view 0, highlighting the wall's thickness.
description_view_3: The other side view, mirroring view 1, with the stepped stone arrangement and visible mortar lines.
attribute_location_context: urban retaining wall or boundary in a city park or along a sidewalk
attribute_weathering_pattern: The surface shows signs of weathering with uneven coloration and slight chipping along some edges, indicating exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous wall
